_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to recognise navigation features in digital texts — like headlines, menus, links and graphics — that make them easier to read and use.

_source.w: We are learning to recognise navigation features in digital texts — like headlines, menus, links and graphics — that make them easier to read and use.
y: Navigating digital texts effectively is essential in our technology-rich world.
Understanding these features helps us find information quickly and evaluate digital sources.
This skill helps us create our own digital texts that are user-friendly.
eg: We explore a website and identify all the navigation features — links, menus, search bars, headings.
We discuss how graphics and layout help us understand digital content.
We compare the navigation features of two websites and evaluate which is easier to use.
We use drop-down menus and links to find specific information on a website.
We discuss how digital navigation differs from navigating a printed text.
We design a simple web page layout that includes effective navigation features.
